title: Failing to Perform Hajj While Able
subtitle: Neglecting the Last Pillar of Islam Despite Having the Means

verses.arabic: وَلِلَّهِ عَلَى النَّاسِ حِجُّ الْبَيْتِ مَنِ اسْتَطَاعَ إِلَيْهِ سَبِيلًا ۚ وَمَن كَفَرَ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ غَنِيٌّ عَنِ الْعَالَمِينَ
verses.translation: And [due] to Allah from the people is a pilgrimage to the House — for whoever is able to find thereto a way. But whoever disbelieves — then indeed, Allah is free from need of the worlds.

verses.arabic: وَأَذِّن فِي النَّاسِ بِالْحَجِّ يَأْتُوكَ رِجَالًا وَعَلَىٰ كُلِّ ضَامِرٍ يَأْتِينَ مِن كُلِّ فَجٍّ عَمِيقٍ
verses.translation: And proclaim to the people the Hajj [pilgrimage]; they will come to you on foot and on every lean camel; they will come from every distant pass.
And complete the Hajj and 'umrah for Allah.

brief_explanation: Hajj is the only pillar of Islam where Allah's Quran directly ties its abandonment to an implied warning of kufr — 'and whoever disbelieves, then indeed Allah is free from need of the worlds' (3:97), a statement of terrifying severity. The obligation is binding once in a lifetime upon every Muslim who has the physical ability and financial means, and delaying it without reason when one is capable amounts to willful defiance of an explicit divine command. The Prophet (peace be upon him) warned that one who dies without performing hajj while having no preventing excuse dies resembling a Christian or a Jew. Abandoning hajj out of worldly distraction or procrastination is treating a pillar of Islam as optional — a grave heedlessness of one's standing before Allah.
capital_punishment: There is no hadd (fixed corporal punishment) for abandoning hajj. It is treated as a major sin that requires sincere repentance and performance of hajj at the earliest possibility. If one dies having willfully neglected it while being able, they die with this sin on their account. The Islamic state may compel those who refuse to perform hajj without excuse to do so, and scholars have noted it can be an act of apostasy if one openly denies its obligation.
